meanings.definition: change the arrangement or position of
destroy the peace or tranquility of
examples: Don't interrupt me when I'm reading

meanings.definition: damage as if by shaking or jarring
examples: Don't disturb the patient's wounds by moving him too rapidly!

meanings.definition: having the place or position changed
examples: the disturbed books and papers on her desk
disturbed grass showed where the horse had passed

meanings.definition: afflicted with or marked by anxious uneasiness or trouble or grief
examples: distressed about her son's leaving home
one last worried check of the sleeping children

meanings.definition: emotionally unstable and having difficulty coping with personal relationships

examples: a man who had gone mad
